name: MacTutor
biography:
Niels Nielsen 's father was a farmer and the family were quite poor. Perhaps as a consequence of his background, he began his time at high school intending to study practical subjects. He had decided to enter the Polytechnic Institute to begin these studies but while still at school developed a love of pure mathematics so decided that it would be more appropriate for him to study at university.
Nielsen entered the University of Copenhagen in 1885 and graduated from there in 1891 . In fact he had begun to teach in secondary schools in 1887 while still working for his degree at the University of Copenhagen and he continued teaching while working for his doctorate which was awarded in 1895 . He gave preparatory courses for the Polytechnic Institute beginning in 1900 and from 1903 until 1906 he was on the University Inspectorate for Secondary Schools. Nielsen became a university teacher in 1905 and he succeeded Petersen as professor at Copenhagen in 1909 .
He wrote on special functions , particularly the gamma function , building on theory introduced by Jensen . Early papers which he published while still teaching in schools include: Sur le produit de deux fonctions cylindriques 창혫혟 ( On the product of two cylindrical functions ) (1899) ; Sur la d횄짤veloppement du z횄짤ro en s횄짤ries de fonctions cylindriques 창혫혟 ( On the development of zero series of cylindrical functions ) (1899) ; Recherches sur les s횄짤ries de fonctions cylindriques dues 횄혻 C Neumann et W Kapteyn 창혫혟 ( Research series of cylindrical functions due to C. Neumann and W Kapteyn ) (1901) ; Note sur la convergence d'une s횄짤rie neumannienne de fonctions cylindriques 창혫혟 ( Note on the convergence of a series of Neumann's cylindrical functions ) (1901) ; and Recherches sur les s횄짤ries de factorielles 창혫혟 ( Researches on the series of factorials ) (1902) .
In 1904 he published rather a large number of works including the papers Sur une int횄짤grale d횄짤finie; Note sur les s횄짤ries de fonctions bernoulliennes 창혫혟 ( On a definite integral; Note the series of functions bernoulliennes ) ; and Les s횄짤ries de factorielles et les op횄짤rations fondamentales 창혫혟 ( Series of factorials and the fundamental operations ) . In the same year he published the widely used text Handbuch der Theorie der Zylinderfunktionen 창혫혟 ( Handbook of the theory of cylindrical functions ) which gave formulas for partial derivatives of Bessel functions with respect to order at integral values. Two years later he published another widely used text Handbuch der Theorie der Gammafunktion 창혫혟 ( Handbook of theory of the gamma function ) ( Teubner, Leipzig, 1906) in which he brought together work which he had completed over the preceeding twenty years. It was the first major work devoted to the study of the gamma function since a treatise by Legendre . In the same year he published Theorie des Integrallogarithmus und verwandter Transzendenten 창혫혟 ( Theory of the logarithmic integral and related transcendent functions ) ( Teubner, Leipzig, 1906) . These two texts were reprinted by the Chelsea Publishing Company of New York in 1965 , bound into a single volume.
In the couple of years before World War I, Nielsen published papers such as Recherches sur le d횄짤veloppement d'une fonction analytique en s횄짤rie de fonctions hyperg횄짤ometriques 창혫혟 ( Research on the development of an analytic function in series of hypergeometric functions ) (1913) and Recherches sur les r횄짤sidus quadratiques et sur les quotients de Fermat 창혫혟 ( Research on quadratic residues and Fermat quotients ) (1914) . During the war, however, in 1917 , he suffered a breakdown from which he never fully recovered. He did, however, continue to produce quality mathematics books. He turned to number theory and studied Bernoulli numbers in Trait횄짤 횄짤l횄짤mentaire des nombres de Bernoulli 창혫혟 ( Elementary treatise of the Bernoulli numbers ) ( Gauthier-Villars, Paris, 1923) and Fermat 's equation writing good textbooks on these topics. His textbooks, however, contained little original mathematics but they were well written texts with much skill in organising the material.
He also wrote two books on the history of Danish mathematics and two books on the history of French mathematics [ 1 ] :- ... he occupied himself primarily with accounts of personalities and the historical development of specific problems. For example he wrote the two volume text on Danish mathematics, the first volume covering the years 1528 to 1800 , and the second volume covering the yeras 1801 to 1908 . It was published in 1910 . G횄짤om횄짢tres fran횄짠ais sous la r횄짤volution 창혫혟 ( French Surveyors in the revolution ) , containing biographies of 76 mathematicians was published in 1929 ( and has proved very useful to the authors of this archive ) and G횄짤om횄짢tres fran횄짠ais du dix-huiti횄짢me si횄짢cle 창혫혟 ( French Surveyors of the eighteenth century ) , containing biographies of 153 mathematicians, was published after Nielsen's death.
Oettel writes in [ 1 ] :- He was a master in the treatment of unmethodical calculations and came up with a multitude of particular points. He playfully conceived new things that were not always in a complete form, and he was a significant influence on his students.
